action.reasoning: Observation: the last iteration failed due to two issues: a shell syntax error prevented the local predict smoke test from running, and model loading still mismatches legacy checkpoint architectures (kernel width 2 vs 3, horizon params shape [12] vs [1,12,1]). Analysis: this is now clearly a pure compatibility problem, not a modeling/training problem. We already have strong checkpoints, especially ema_weighted.pt, so the highest-value move is to fully fix model.py to infer exact legacy variants from state_dict shapes and load them losslessly, then locally smoke test both ema_weighted.pt and fixed_retrain.pt and submit the best loadable checkpoint immediately. Decision: rewrite model.py with exact variant inference for legacy checkpoints, keep predict.py simple, run local tests without fragile heredoc chaining, and submit ema_weighted.pt once loading is verified.
